MCE.description: Integrated suite of instruments monitoring the near-space environment, combining multiple measurements into a unified orbital dataset.
MCE.hints: Generate broad, survey-style observations of the space environment: radiation background, microgravity conditions, particulate matter, and long-term trends. Results should feel generic, cumulative, and comparative between low and high orbit rather than focused on a single phenomenon.

SEDA.description: Continuous monitoring of the orbital space environment, recording radiation, energetic particles, and environmental hazards affecting spacecraft and crews.
SEDA.hints: Emphasize space weather, radiation exposure, and transient events (solar activity, particle bursts). Contrast InSpaceLow partial shielding versus InSpaceHigh exposure. Keep the tone operational and hazard-aware, suitable for assessing risks to long-duration missions.
